brief_document.title: NIF registration surface
brief_document.purpose: Provide the surface through which host-supplied NIFs and the engine's own NIFs are registered with beamr's native registry before any workflow runs. Workflow code reaches Rust through these NIFs; the builder collects them and this module installs them.
brief_document.task: Implement the runtime NIF registration surface: a type that accumulates NIF entries (the engine's own plus host-supplied) and installs them into beamr's native registry via the RuntimeHandle. Registration must run before any workflow spawns, and a duplicate MFA must surface as EngineError::NifRegistration. This crate only registers NIFs; it does not provide authoring macros (that is aion-nif, cluster AN).

requirements.title: NIF entry collection
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define a NIF-registration surface that collects native function entries keyed by module/function/arity, accepting both the engine's own NIFs and host-supplied NIFs handed in via the builder.
acceptance: nif.rs defines a NifRegistration (or equivalent) that holds a collection of native entries, each carrying an MFA key, a beamr-compatible native function, and a dirty-scheduler flag.
The surface exposes a method to add a host-supplied set of NIFs and a method to add the engine's own NIFs.
The type is constructible empty and is consumed (not borrowed indefinitely) at install time so entries cannot be added after registration.

requirements.title: Install into beamr before any workflow runs
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL install all collected NIF entries into beamr's native registry through the RuntimeHandle during engine build, before any workflow process is spawned. IF a duplicate module/function/arity is registered, THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL return EngineError::NifRegistration and SHALL NOT spawn any workflow.
acceptance: A RuntimeHandle method installs every collected entry into beamr's native registry, honoring the dirty-scheduler flag per entry.
Registration is invoked during build (AE-010 wires the call); this brief provides the install method and its error contract.
A duplicate-MFA registration returns EngineError::NifRegistration { reason } carrying the offending MFA, mapping beamr's NativeRegistrationError::DuplicateMfa.
A unit test registers two NIFs with the same MFA and asserts EngineError::NifRegistration; a second test registers distinct NIFs and asserts they are callable from a trivial test module.

boundaries: SHALL NOT provide NIF-authoring macros or term-mapping helpers — that is aion-nif (cluster AN).
SHALL NOT define which concrete NIFs workflows call (signal/timer/query NIFs are AT; activity NIFs are supplied by the host or aion-nif).
SHALL NOT spawn workflows or wire the builder — registration is invoked by build in AE-010.
verification: Duplicate-MFA path returns EngineError::NifRegistration with no panic.

checklist.text: A NIF registration surface accepts host-supplied NIFs plus the engine's own NIFs and registers them with beamr's native registry before any workflow runs; duplicate-MFA registration is surfaced as EngineError::NifRegistration.

stories.text: As an engine operator, I want to build an Engine from a store, loaded workflows, and my NIFs with one builder so that embedding durable workflows in my Rust app is a single, clear assembly step.

constraints.text: unsafe_code = deny. No unsafe in the crate.
No #[allow]/#[expect]/#[ignore] lint bypasses per CLAUDE.md; tests needing a runtime gate it at runtime, never #[ignore].
lib.rs/mod.rs are declarations and re-exports only.
aion depends only on aion-core, aion-store, aion-package, and beamr among workspace crates — no aion-server, no store backend crate, no networking crate. Structural; must hold.
Transport-agnostic: no HTTP, gRPC, or WebSocket dependency or code anywhere in the crate.
Only the runtime module imports beamr; every other module uses the RuntimeHandle (per D1).
All library errors are thiserror EngineError variants; no anyhow in this library crate. No .unwrap()/.expect() outside tests.
Mutex/RwLock poison handled explicitly and mapped to a typed EngineError; no .unwrap() on a lock guard.
WorkflowStatus is read from aion-core's event projection; the registry's cached status is never the source of truth (per aion-core CO7).
aion hardcodes no scheduler thread count, no timeout, and no retry default; configurable values come from the builder or are deferred to beamr's own default.
Module namespacing uses aion-package's transform unchanged; the engine never re-derives namespaced names (per D8).
Engine tests run against InMemoryStore; no test requires a database (per aion-core D8).

resolved_context.intention: Build the heart of the engine — the aion crate you embed. Given an EventStore, a set of loaded .aion workflow packages, and registered NIFs, EngineBuilder produces a live Engine that starts workflows, runs each as a BEAM process on an embedded beamr runtime, supervises them through a three-level tree, dispatches their activities as linked child processes, and cancels or reports on them by ID. Starting a workflow is spawning a process; cancelling it is killing that process, which through links kills its activity children too; an activity crash propagates an exit signal the trapping workflow process receives as a message. The crate is transport-agnostic — no HTTP/gRPC/WebSocket — keeping the embedded path free of network dependencies. This cluster owns lifecycle, process management, supervision, and module loading; it defers durability/replay (AD) and time/signals/queries/children/concurrency (AT) to their owners, surfacing their entry points on Engine and stating the seams explicitly.

provenance.quote: Build the heart of the engine — the aion crate you embed. Given an EventStore, a set of loaded .aion workflow packages, and registered NIFs, EngineBuilder produces a live Engine that starts workflows,
